name: Imran Khan Cancer Appeal USA
mission: TO GENERATE CHARITABLE DONATIONS FOR HELPING IN THE TREATMENT OF CANCER IN THIRD WORLD COUNTRIES AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF THE SHAUKAT KHANUM MEMORIAL CANCER HOSPITAL AND RESEARCH CENTER. SPECIFIC GOALS INCLUDE (1) TO INITIATE CHARITABLE FUND RAISING ACTIVITIES, (2) TO RECRUIT PROFESSIONAL AND NON- PROFESSIONAL STAFF AND EXPENSES FOR THE ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE, (3) TO SUPPLY FINANCIAL SUPPORT FOR THE PAYMENT OF MEDICINES, DIAGNOSTIC AND THERAPEUTIC EQUIPMENT FOR THE SHAUKAT KHANUM MEMORIAL CANCER HOSPITAL AND RESEARCH CENTER. (4) TO ASSIST IN THE RECRUITMENT OF ALL MEDICAL AND ANCILLARY PERSONNEL FOR THE SHAUKAT KHANUM MEMORIAL CANCER HOSPITAL AND RESEARCH CENTER.
programs: Cancer Registry and Clinical Data Management
scoreSummary: Imran Khan Cancer Appeal, Inc. shows strong alignment and moderate impact, with zakat compliance (major risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.shaukatkhanum.org.pk/zakat (Source: https://www.shaukatkhanum.org.pk/zakat)

judge_issues.message: The rationale states "elite financial efficiency" which is not supported by the data. The financial efficiency is good, but the board size is a major risk factor that is not mentioned in the rationale.
The impact rationale does not mention the small board size, which is a major factor in the risk deduction.
Website shows related EIN (92-5905000), likely parent/subsidiary of 13-3626299

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The charity demonstrates elite financial efficiency and strong alignment with donor values, though its overall score is moderated by a need for more robust impact data.

dimension_explanations.alignment: There is high alignment for donors seeking to support global health, as the charity is Zakat-eligible and serves urgent medical needs in Pakistan [2][4]. The organization's track record in oncology services provides a strong fit for those prioritizing healthcare infrastructure [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization focuses on capacity building through clinical and diagnostic services, maintaining a high Charity Navigator score of 95.0/100 [1]. While it reports a total revenue of $23,259,494 and a 97.4% program expense ratio, specific cost-per-beneficiary data is not fully disclosed [1][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: Imran Khan Cancer Appeal, Inc. provides critical cancer treatment and diagnostic services to underserved populations in developing nations.
strengths: High fundraising efficiency with only $0.01 spent per $1 raised
Strong financial health with 9.8 months of working capital
baseline_narrative.summary: The organization generates charitable donations to support the Shaukat Khanum Memorial Cancer Hospital and Research Centre, focusing on providing medical care to patients in third-world countries [3]. It is recognized as a Zakat-eligible charity, maintaining a formal policy to ensure funds reach those in need [2][4].

case_against.summary: The primary concern is the <cite id="12">minimal board size</cite> and the absence of <cite id="7">rigorous external impact evaluations</cite>.

confidence.data_freshness: Financial data from 2023, impact data from 2019 report
confidence.notes: Strong financial data but limited governance and outcome depth.

alignment.explanation: The charity is a strong fit for Muslim donors due to its <cite id="6">verified zakat program</cite> and focus on <cite id="14">underserved populations in Pakistan</cite>.
alignment.improvement: Expanding services to <cite id="15">additional underserved geographies</cite> would further enhance its alignment with global humanitarian needs.

credibility.explanation: IKCA is highly credible in its financial reporting, earning a <cite id="8">95/100 rating from Charity Navigator</cite> and maintaining <cite id="13">97.4% independent board members</cite> at the operational level.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the charity should <cite id="12">expand its board size</cite> beyond a single member to ensure robust governance.

impact.explanation: Impact is driven by a <cite id="2">100.0% program ratio</cite> and specialized services like <cite id="10">pharmacy and cancer registry management</cite>.
impact.improvement: The charity should <cite id="7">publish consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for a formal cost-effectiveness assessment.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade C - Internal tracking without external validation

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Directly funds the operations of its namesake hospital in Pakistan

rich_narrative.headline: Imran Khan Cancer Appeal, Inc. provides critical cancer treatment and diagnostic services to underserved populations in developing nations.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to fulfill zakat obligations by funding high-tech medical interventions for cancer patients in Pakistan with maximum financial efficiency.
donor_motivations: Want to ensure 97.4% of their donation reaches the program
Prioritize cancer treatment as a critical health gap
Trust established institutions with a 30-year track record
giving_considerations: The charity has a very small board which may concern those focused on governance
Impact data is primarily focused on outputs (cases treated) rather than long-term survival rates
Focus is geographically limited to Pakistan
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking organizations with large, diverse governing boards or those requiring independent RCT-level impact data.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects consistent internal reporting of <cite id="11">6,446 malignant neoplasms treated</cite> but a lack of independent verification.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: IKCA funds specialized cancer facilities to provide free or subsidized treatment to those who cannot afford it, aiming to reduce cancer mortality in Pakistan.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Tracking outcomes ensures that high-cost cancer treatments are resulting in successful patient remissions.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="4">26.2% growth rate</cite> and expanding clinical services, the charity can absorb significant new capital for hospital expansion.

peer_comparison.differentiator: IKCA's <cite id="2">100.0% program ratio</cite> is significantly higher than the <cite id="16">peer median of 88.2%</cite>.

strengths.detail: The charity directs a <cite id="2">97.4% program expense ratio</cite> toward its mission, ensuring zero leakage to administrative overhead in its primary filings.
IKCA holds an <cite id="8">overall score of 95.0/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting strong leadership and financial health.
The organization maintains <cite id="9">9.8 months of working capital</cite> in working capital, providing a significant buffer for emergency medical needs.

strengths_deep_dive: IKCA's financial model is built on extreme lean operations. By spending only <cite id="3">$0.02 to raise every dollar</cite>, the organization maximizes the impact of individual contributions. This efficiency has allowed them to scale rapidly, as evidenced by their <cite id="4">26.2% revenue growth rate</cite> over the last three years. Their focus on <cite id="10">clinical and diagnostic services</cite> in Pakistan addresses a critical gap in global health, specifically for <cite id="11">pediatric cancer cases</cite> and malignant neoplasms.
rich_narrative.summary: Imran Khan Cancer Appeal, Inc. (IKCA) serves as a vital financial lifeline for the Shaukat Khanum Memorial Cancer Hospital and Research Centre, providing <cite id="1">critical cancer treatment and diagnostic services</cite> to underserved populations in Pakistan. The organization demonstrates exceptional financial discipline, maintaining a <cite id="2">97.4% program expense ratio</cite> that ensures nearly every dollar donated directly supports medical care. This efficiency is further highlighted by its <cite id="3">fundraising efficiency of $0.01 per $1 raised</cite>, a figure that significantly outperforms industry standards. Over the last three years, IKCA has shown robust growth with a <cite id="4">3-year revenue CAGR of 26.2%</cite>, reaching <cite id="5">$17,974,012 in total revenue</cite> by 2023. Beyond its financial health, the charity is a cornerstone for Muslim donors, offering <cite id="6">verified zakat eligibility</cite> and serving specific asnaf categories including the poor and needy. While the organization operates with high financial transparency, its governance structure is lean, and it currently lacks <cite id="7">third-party impact evaluations</cite> to verify long-term cost-effectiveness per patient.
